items.title: Taijul hopeful improving wicket can spark Bangladesh comeback
items.summary: Innocent Kaia's maiden Test century put Zimbabwe firmly in control, despite Taijul Islam returning a record-equalling seven-wicket haul.

items.title: Kaia's maiden ton, Madhevere and Ervine's fifties extend Zimbabwe's dominance
items.summary: After Zimbabwe were bowled out for 410, with Taijul picking up a seven-for, Bangladesh lost Shadman in the second innings

items.title: Kaia ton leads Zimbabwe charge as hosts tighten grip on Day 2
items.summary: Half-centuries from Bennett, Ervine and Madhevere helped Zimbabwe secure a 270-run lead despite Taijul Islam's 7-fer

items.title: Taijul shines but Zimbabwe in the driver's seat
items.summary: With three days remaining, Bangladesh still trail by 230 runs and face a daunting task to save the Test.

items.title: Litton likely to return for second ODI in Zimbabwe
items.summary: Litton is expected to recover from his calf injury in time for the second and third ODIs in Zimbabwe and play "if he can meet all the parameters of our fitness test"

items.title: Pacers, openers complete Zimbabwe's perfect day
items.summary: The hosts bowled Bangladesh out for 140 and were only four behind by stumps on day one

items.title: Tamim inducted as MCC honorary life member
items.summary: New Zealand women's cricket greats Suzie Bates and Sophie Devine, Australia's Mel Jones and India's Cheteshwar Pujara have also been awarded the honour.

items.title: Tigresses sign off with defeat, secure direct qualification for 2028
items.summary: Despite a spirited bowling effort while defending a below-par total, Bangladesh suffered a four-wicket defeat to South Africa in their final Group A match, ending their ICC Women's T20 World Cup campaign on a losing note at Lord's on Sunday.

items.title: South Africa seal nervy chase against Bangladesh and wait for Australia vs India
items.summary: If Australia beat India, South Africa will qualify for the semi-finals

items.title: Bangladesh opt to bat, SA unchanged in high-stakes game
items.summary: Bangladesh brought in Taj Nehar for Dilara Akter at the top of the order

items.title: Australia hold the key as India, South Africa chase semi-final spots
items.summary: South Africa are the favourites to beat Bangladesh, but their poor net run rate means their chances hinge on Australia beating India

items.title: Zimbabwe opt to bowl; Bangladesh hand debuts to Amite Hasan and Towhid Hridoy
items.summary: For Zimbabwe, Wessly Madhevere took the spot of Sikandar Raza but there was no room for Graeme Cremer

items.title: Zimbabwe look to build on rare Test momentum against Bangladesh
items.summary: Ngarava begins his captaincy stint as Zimbabwe seek to back up last year's Sylhet win over a confident Bangladesh
